title: Number of people living in extreme poverty
subtitle: Extreme poverty is defined as living below the [International Poverty Line](#dod:international-poverty-line) of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Number of people living in extreme poverty, World, 1990 to 2026

Extreme poverty is defined as living below the International Poverty Line of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This stacked area chart tracks the global population distribution between those living in extreme poverty and those not, spanning from 1990 to 2026. The vertical axis measures the number of people in billions, while the horizontal axis marks time in five-year increments. The lower section, shaded in dark pink, represents the population living in extreme poverty, while the upper section, shaded in light orange, represents the population not in extreme poverty. In 1990, the number of people living in extreme poverty stood at approximately 2.3 billion. This figure remained relatively stable through the mid-1990s before beginning a consistent downward trajectory. By 2010, the count had fallen to roughly 1.5 billion. The decline continued through 2015 and 2020, reaching a level below 1 billion by the end of the observed period. Conversely, the population not living in extreme poverty shows a continuous upward trend throughout the entire timeline. Starting at roughly 3 billion in 1990, this group expanded to approximately 6 billion by 2010 and is projected to exceed 7 billion by 2026. The total height of the chart rises steadily, indicating overall global population growth during these decades. The data is expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices and relates to income measured after taxes and benefits or to consumption per capita. The visual gap between the two colored areas widens significantly over time, showing that the share of the global population escaping extreme poverty has grown alongside the total population.
